name: Centipede
description:
Centipede is a fixed shooter game where the player controls a character at the bottom of the screen. The primary objective is to eliminate a segmented centipede that descends from the top of the screen, moving horizontally until it hits the edge of the screen, then drops down one level and reverses direction. The centipede breaks into smaller segments when shot. Each segment then becomes a mushroom. 

Mushrooms are scattered throughout the playfield and serve multiple purposes. They obstruct the player's movement, impede the centipede's descent (causing it to drop down a level when it collides with one), and are created when the centipede is shot. More mushrooms appear randomly throughout the game. 

Fleas occasionally appear from the top of the screen, dropping mushrooms behind them as they descend, creating obstacles for the player. Spiders crawl across the bottom area of the screen in unpredictable patterns, sometimes moving upward into the playfield. They are worth different point values depending on how close they are to the player. 

Scorpions occasionally appear on the side of the screen and move horizontally across the playfield. When the scorpion stings a mushroom, that mushroom becomes poisonous. If the centipede touches a poisonous mushroom, it plummets straight down to the player's area. 

The game ends when the player runs out of lives. Each time the player gets hit by the centipede, a flea, a spider or a scorpion, a life is lost. Points are awarded for shooting the centipede, fleas, scorpions, and spiders. Killing an entire centipede segment grants a bonus. Different enemies yield different point values. The gameplay becomes progressively harder as the levels increase, with more centipedes, faster movement, and more frequent appearances of enemies.
